title: Step 1: Link Datadog to your project using your Datadog  key.

sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To integrate Atlas with Datadog, you must have [Project Owner](<Project Owner>) access
to the project. Users with [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) access must add
themselves to the project as a `Project Owner`.

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
Datadog integration is available only on `M10+` clusters.

To integrate Atlas with Datadog, you must have a Datadog account
and a Datadog API (Application Programming Interface) key. Datadog grants you an API (Application Programming Interface) key when you first
create a Datadog account.

If you do not have an existing Datadog account, you can sign up at
[https://app.datadoghq.com/signup](https://app.datadoghq.com/signup).

sections.heading: Procedure
sections.body:
Atlas CLI

To create or update a Datadog integration using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas integrations create DATADOG [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas integrations create DATADOG](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-integrations-create-DATADOG).

Atlas UI

sections.heading: Procedure
sections.body:
To configure an Atlas integration with Datadog using the
Atlas UI:

- Click Configure for the Datadog integration card.

- Enter your Datadog API (Application Programming Interface) key in the input box.

- Select your API (Application Programming Interface) region.

  Atlas supports the following Datadog regions in the
  Atlas UI:

  - `US1`

  - `US3`

  - `US5`

  - `EU1`

  - `AP1`

  Datadog uses `US1` by default.

  To learn more about Datadog's regions, see [Datadog Sites](https://docs.datadoghq.com/getting_started/site/).

- (Optional) To enable database metrics tracking, toggle
  Send Database Metrics to On.

- (Optional) To enable collection latency metrics tracking,
  toggle Send Collection Latency Metrics to
  On.

- (Optional) To enable query shape metrics tracking,
  toggle Send Query Shape Metrics to
  On.

- Click Save.

sections.heading: Performance Metrics Available to Datadog
sections.body:
Datadog tracks the following metric data for your Atlas
cluster. The metric names in parentheses are the names used in
the Datadog UI (User Interface).

> **Important:** By default, Datadog does not track high cardinality metric data.
> To enable tracking for these metrics, you must specify the following settings:
>
> - `sendDatabaseMetrics`: Tracks database metrics, including
>   database names, collection numbers, storage size, and index size.
>   These metrics have names prefixed with `mongodb.atlas.dbstats`.
>
> - `sendCollectionLatencyMetrics`: Tracks collection latency metrics,
>   including database names, collection names, and latency information about
>   commands, operations, and transactions. These metrics have names
>   prefixed with `mongodb.atlas.latencyStats`.
>
> - `sendQueryShapeMetrics`: Track query shape metrics including
>   execution time and count, keys and documents examined and
>   returned, and response time.  These metrics have names
>   prefixed with `mongodb.atlas.querystats`.
>
> Enable these settings by using an [Atlas Administration API endpoint](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-creategroupintegration)
> or through the [the Procedure section in this document](datadog-procedure).

sections.heading: Tags Available to Datadog
sections.body:
The integration creates the following
[Datadog tags](https://docs.datadoghq.com/getting_started/tagging/)
on certain metrics:

| Datadog Tags              | Description                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| ------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| organizationnameorg\_name | Atlas organization associated with the metric.                                                                                                                                               |
| projectnamegroup\_name    | Atlas project associated with the metric.                                                                                                                                                    |
| group\_id                 | Unique identifier of the Atlas project associated with the metric.                                                                                                                           |
| clustername               | Atlas cluster associated with the metric.                                                                                                                                                    |
| replicasetname            | Replica set associated with the metric.                                                                                                                                                      |
| shardedclustername        | Sharded cluster associated with the metric.                                                                                                                                                  |
| databasename              | Database associated with the metric.                                                                                                                                                         |
| collectionname            | Collection associated with the metric.                                                                                                                                                       |
| hostnameport              | Port number associated with your Atlas hostname.                                                                                                                                             |
| hostnamestate             | State of your Atlas hostname (primary, secondary, or individual process). You can use this tag to filter collection-level latency metrics when querying your sharded cluster or replica set. |
| processor\_name           | Name of the stream processor associated with the metric.                                                                                                                                     |
| processor\_id             | Unique identifier of the stream processor associated with the metric.                                                                                                                        |
| tenant\_name              | Name of the stream processing workspace associated with the metric.                                                                                                                          |
| tenant\_id                | Unique identifier of the stream processing workspace associated with the metric.                                                                                                             |

In addition, Atlas sends user-defined [custom tags](configure-resource-tags) to Datadog. Tags apply only to metrics, not
alerts.

> **Note:** Sending custom tags to Datadog metrics is an opt-in feature that you enable through the
> UI or the API. See [the Procedure section in this document](datadog-procedure) to
> learn more about each procedure.

sections.heading: Datadog Features for Tracking Your Atlas Integration Status
sections.body:
Datadog provides the following features that you can set up to track
the status of your Atlas integration:

sections.heading: No-data Alert
sections.body:
[No data alerts in Datadog](https://docs.datadoghq.com/monitors/configuration/?tab=thresholdalert#no-data)
notify you when a metric is not reporting data under normal
circumstances. This feature helps you identify issues with your
Atlas integration such as changes in performance, API Key issues, or broken connections.

sections.heading: Service Checks
sections.body:
[Service Checks in Datadog](https://docs.datadoghq.com/developers/service_checks/#overview)
monitor the status of your Atlas integration with Datadog.
This feature monitors whether a consistent cadence metric is unavailable for a
specified number of consecutive times. If the Service Check sends an alert,
your Atlas integration has an issue.
